Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First Ill look at the data for each case Lets start with the preliminary scores and the adjustments

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at Case C001 The LocalResident is TRUE and theyre part of the Highland Civic Darts Club Their preliminary score is 710 and then theres a plus 10 adjustment for Community making the adjusted score 720 They get approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C002 LocalResident is TRUE but no community group Preliminary score 720 adjustment 0 They also get approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C003 LocalResident is FALSE no community group Preliminary score 715 adjustment 0 Rejected

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C004 LocalResident TRUE part of the same club as C001 Preliminary 690 adjustment plus 10 adjusted 700 Approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C005 LocalResident FALSE no community group Preliminary 740 adjustment 0 Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the key here is the Community adjustment For those in a community group they get a plus 10

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Wait how does the final decision get made The Rules Engine uses the adjusted score The threshold for approval isnt given but maybe the adjusted score needs to be above a certain number For example maybe 700 is the cutoff

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Lets check C001 720 adjusted Approved C002 720 Approved C003 715 Rejected C004 700 Approved C005 740 Approved

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: So C003 was rejected with 715 which is higher than C004s 700 So the cutoff might be around 700 or higher But C003 was rejected even though their score is higher than C004s adjusted Thats odd Maybe theres another factor

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Wait C003s LocalResident is FALSE Maybe the LocalResident status also affects the decision

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But in C005 LocalResident is FALSE but they got approved with 740

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: So maybe LocalResident isnt a direct factor but combined with other factors

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The key bias here is the Community adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If a person is part of a community group their score is increased by 10

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be a form of bias because its an external factor not directly related to creditworthiness The system is giving an advantage based on community membership which might correlate with geography or social connections thus disadvantaging those not in such groups

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a disparity where individuals in those groups get a higher score potentially leading to approval even if their base score is lower

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This could perpetuate existing inequalities favoring those with community ties and possibly geographic proximity to certain areas while those without such affiliations are at a disadvantage regardless of their actual creditworthiness
